text: Abstract Abstract Abstract Abstract Abstract Epoxides are important precursors to many pharmaceuticals and epoxy resins, etc. In this study, a Br − /BrO − mediated photoelectrochemical system for the selective and efficient photoelectrochemical epoxidation of α-Fe 2 O 3 towards α-unsaturated aldehydes, naphthalene derivatives, alkyl oleate esters, alkyl fumarate esters, arylalkenes, styrene and acetophenone was established by adjusting the Br − /BrO − concentration, light intensity, alkaline solution concentration and pH in aqueous solution. High selectivity (up to >99%) and faradaic efficiency (up to 82 ± 4%) were achieved for the targets within a broad range of alkenes with water as oxygen source, which is far beyond the most reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical epoxidation performances. Moreover, the Br − /BrO − mediated pathway for the photoelectrochemical selective and efficient epoxidation of alkenes has been verified. Br − is oxidized non-radically by an oxygen atom transfer pathway to produce BrO − on the α-Fe 2 O 3 photoanode surface, and the as-formed BrO − is responsible for the subsequent oxygen atom transfer to alkenes for producing targeted epoxides. The non-radical mediated characteristic and favorable thermodynamics of the oxygen atom transfer contribute significantly to the high efficiency of the photoelectrochemical selective epoxidation of alkenes, thereby providing a new photoelectrochemical Br − /BrO − -mediated method for efficient value-added production of various chemicals such as hydrogen and epoxides.
explanation: The text starts with repeated 'Abstract' tokens and contains slightly awkward phrasing and inconsistencies (e.g., epoxidation yielding α-unsaturated aldehydes), which are typical of AI-generated drafts, despite plausible technical content.
